features.summary: Apple reported Q4 FY2021 revenue of $83.4 billion, up 29% year-over-year, setting September quarter records despite $6 billion in supply constraints from chip shortages and COVID-related manufacturing disruptions. iPhone revenue grew 47% to $38.9 billion, Mac hit an all-time record of $9.2 billion, and Services reached a record $18.3 billion with 26% growth. The company achieved over 20% growth across all product categories and geographic segments, with paid subscriptions reaching 745 million across the platform.
features.management_outlook: Management expects very solid year-over-year revenue growth and a new December quarter record despite larger supply constraints than the $6 billion experienced in Q4. They project revenue growth in all product categories except iPad, which will decline due to supply constraints. Services growth is expected to decelerate but remain strong, with gross margins guided between 41.5% and 42.5%. COVID-related manufacturing disruptions have improved materially through October, though chip shortages on legacy nodes continue to impact most products.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints are expected to worsen in the December quarter, primarily driven by industry-wide chip shortages on legacy nodes affecting most products. The company faces increased freight costs and inflationary pressures on components. Regulatory scrutiny around App Store policies and potential changes to commission structures could impact services revenue growth rates. COVID-related disruptions remain unpredictable and could worsen from current projections.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand across all product categories is driving robust performance, with the iPhone 13 family receiving enthusiastic customer response and the installed base reaching new all-time highs. The Services business benefits from growing paid subscriptions (up 160 million year-over-year), increased customer engagement, and expansion into new offerings like Apple Music Voice. New product launches including M1 Pro/Max MacBook Pro, redesigned AirPods, and Apple Watch Series 7 are expected to drive holiday season demand despite supply constraints.
